markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create fixtures for backlinks, unlinked mentions, tags, folder color labels, graph filters, stale wiki, and exact LoomBlock opens. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LoomNavigationFixtures leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LoomNavigationFixtures is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Consolidated navigation fixture exercising EVERY Loom navigation path over one realistic workspace on real PostgreSQL, proving the MT-177..190 backend surfaces compose over the same authority.

tests: loom_navigation_fixture_exercises_every_path - 10 navigation fixtures in one scenario: (1) exact LoomBlock open; (2) backlinks w/ context snippet; (3) unlinked mentions (excludes formally-linked); (4) tag hub + nested-tag membership; (5) folder tree + color label (#3366ff); (6) breadcrumbs across the spine; (7) local (undirected + tag-only filter) + global graph; (8) pins view w/ order; (9) stale wiki projection that regenerates after a source edit; (10) projection-delete-leaves-block-authority-byte-identical

evidence.summary: A CONCURRENT session's in-flight, non-compiling WIP (knowledge_retrieval MT-133/134 executed-pipeline: graph_planner.rs references KnowledgeEdge.evidence_span_ids which does not exist, + plan/passage_fallback/mod.rs + untracked executor.rs + editor_commands.ts) was present UNCOMMITTED in the product worktree and blocked test compilation. It is NOT WP-009 work and NOT mine. I did not modify or fix it: I backed it up (Handshake_Artifacts/orphan_backups + a git stash kept as a second backup), git-stashed only those paths to run the MT-192 test green, then restored them exactly (stash pop + executor.rs back). The concurrent session's WIP is left as found. WP-009 Loom code compiled green at every one of my commits.

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 20b1f968.

findings: COMPOSITION (handled+tested): every navigation path is exercised against ONE workspace built from shared authority, so the fixture proves the surfaces interoperate (e.g. the same Roadmap block is opened, backlinked, tagged, filed, graphed, pinned, and wiki-cited).
BRIDGE-RESOLVED (handled): every fixture block is bridged to the ProjectKnowledgeIndex so navigation resolves to authority (the entity crumb + citations appear).
AUTHORITY-BOUNDARY (handled+tested): the fixture ends by deleting the wiki projection and asserting the LoomBlock authority is byte-identical (re-proves MT-187 in the composed scenario).
ISOLATION (handled): the fixture uses an isolated per-test schema with the full migration chain (knowledge_pg), no shared state, deterministic assertions.
fixes: No code change required for the fixture. Operator action item (out of WP-009 scope): the concurrent knowledge_retrieval MT-133/134 WIP in the product worktree does not compile (KnowledgeEdge has no evidence_span_ids field; spans live in knowledge_edge_spans) and must be finished or reverted by its owning session before a full-crate build is green.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-192 requires a consolidated Loom navigation fixture exercising every Obsidian-replacement navigation path (exact block open, backlinks, unlinked mentions, tags/nested tags, folder color labels, breadcrumbs, local/global graph filters, pins, stale wiki projection regen, and projection-delete-leaves-authority-intact) over one realistic workspace on real PostgreSQL, proving the surfaces compose over the same authority. The committed test does exactly this against a genuine Handshake-managed/real PostgreSQL harness with isolated schemas and the full migration chain, calling real postgres-backed implementations, and aligns with current Master Spec Section 10.12.
findings: Single commit 20b1f968 adds exactly one new file, src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loom_navigation_fixtures_tests.rs (191 lines), in-scope per packet allowed_paths (tests/). No /.GOV/ or out-of-scope product files touched.

The test loom_navigation_fixture_exercises_every_path (loom_navigation_fixtures_tests.rs:92) builds one workspace and exercises 10 fixtures: exact open (line 112), backlinks+context (116-120), unlinked mentions with linked-exclusion negative assert (123-127), tag hub + nested-tag membership (130-135), folder tree + color label #3366ff (138-149), breadcrumbs spine asserting workspace/folder/project/entity crumbs (152-155), local undirected + tag-only-filtered + global graph (158-165), pins view+order (168-174), stale wiki projection that regenerates after a source edit (177-184), and projection-delete-leaves-block-byte-identical (187-190).

All called methods resolve to real postgres-backed implementations, not stubs: scan_unlinked_mentions at postgres.rs:9593 is genuine SQL (QueryBuilder with NOT EXISTS edge-exclusion + ILIKE prefilter); get_backlinks_with_context at postgres.rs:9544; local_graph at 9709; loom_wiki_projection_is_stale at 10459; regenerate_loom_wiki_projection (api/loom.rs:919 wired through storage); loom_block_breadcrumbs at 10704.

Sub-rule 2: the harness (tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-165) connects a real PostgresDatabase to an isolated per-test schema (knowledge_test_<uuidv7>), installs pgcrypto + digest shims, and runs the full migration chain (db.run_migrations()). base_database_url() (line 10) resolves POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL or falls back to ManagedPostgres::ensure_running — real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL lifecycle, not an implementer-authored mock. When no PG is configured it SKIPs (returns early), never a fake pass.
Sub-rule 1: grep over the loom postgres impl range (postgres.rs 9544-10800) found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable on the proof path.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 module 10-product-surfaces.md (Section 10.12 Loom, lines 8534-8550) requires Loom navigation, Obsidian-like backlinks, unlinked/sorted/pinned/backlink graph views as 'a projection over product authority, not a second store'; line 8253 makes PostgreSQL+EventLedger the canonical authority. FIXTURE 10 (projection-delete-leaves-block-byte-identical) directly proves the markdown-vault-never-authority constraint; FIXTURES 2-9 cover the named navigation surfaces.
Sub-rule 3 NA: this is a WP-Validator advisory review and the lifecycle.completed_by is null (READY_FOR_VALIDATION); no self-cert COMPLETED transition was signed by the implementer.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): the evidence block notes a concurrent unrelated knowledge_retrieval MT-133/134 WIP that does not compile; the coder correctly left it as found and it is out of WP-009 scope. Self-review sub-agent verdict carries no authority and was disregarded.
evidence_checked: git show --stat / git log --grep MT-192 in wtc-kernel-009

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
